title: Spawn-packet — Tau price-history recording capability (shipping-app graduation onto substrate's chronon-typed timeline tools)
spawnVariant: spawn-shipping-app-capability-addition (informal — substrate has 5 named variants; this is a 6th shape warranting separate typed promotion)
spawnVariantRationale: Tau is a shipping options-trading app (NOT new spawn); the price-history-recording capability is a substrate-graduation of existing PollingDaemon → CoreData pipeline onto chronon-typed timeline-storage-matrix backend. Not spawn-runnable-app (Tau exists) + not spawn-typed-primitive-light (substrate tools exist already). The substrate's typed-workstream catalog may want a 6th variant — informal-for-now per [[deferral-is-drift-do-it-now]] balanced against turn-scope.
operatorAttestation: i want tau to start recording price history now that we know we have tools which we can begin to use for this...

substrateDoctrinalRecognition.summary: Tau-price-history-recording isn't new substrate work — it's the meeting of two substrate-doctrinal arcs the substrate has been building separately for a year. Arc A: Tau's PollingDaemon-Quote pipeline (year-old; shipping). Arc B: substrate's chronon-schemas family (v0.1.0 → v0.4.0; 9 storage variants benchmarked in timeline-storage-matrix). The substrate just realized the two were always meant to compose.
substrateDoctrinalRecognition.siblingToFoundingConsumerRecognition: [[founding-consumer-precedes-typed-primitive-by-substrate-pacing]] — substrate's year-long doctrine-refinement → typed-primitive-extraction → founding-consumer-graduation cycle. Today's session recognized Tau as the founding consumer of constraint-solver (matrix-solver). This packet recognizes Tau as the founding consumer of chronon-schemas (time-series). Same cycle, different typed primitive, same year-old founding app.

doctrinalBackground.sequenceDoctrine: [[design-and-product-first-code-last]] — packet authors position 1 (Design + Product) prospectively. No code this turn.
doctrinalBackground.checklistDoctrine: [[substrate-modern-composition-checklist-is-required-spawn-software-proof-gate]] — checklist applies if recording surfaces new UI; recording-only is daemon-level work but visualization is checklist-gated
doctrinalBackground.graduationDoctrine: [[time-series-recording-substrate-graduation-strategy]] (just-landed child pattern) — invoked for this packet's storage / schema / integration decisions
doctrinalBackground.preservationDoctrine: [[preserve-prior-schema-versions]] + [[Preserve historical data]] + [[Never trash data under investigation]] — CoreData lineage preserved; chronon-typed graduation honors the year-old pipeline

newSurfaces: tau-app/.../private/apple/kits/TauHistoryKit (new SPM kit — proposed; chronon-typed bridge primitives for Tau)
tau-app/.../catalyst-app/Recording/ (new directory — PollingDaemon write-through callback target)
instances of chronon-typed QuoteMoment records (live; CBL store)
existingSurfacesTouched: tau-app/.../cross/resources/Trader.xcdatamodeld (potentially: add QuoteHistoryTick entity OR leave untouched per hybrid-bridge candidate)
tau-app/.../catalyst-app/QuoteViewModel.swift + FetchedQuotes.swift (read-path bridge to chronon-typed store for charts)

childPatternRefs.t: Primary child pattern this packet invokes
Composes — storage-tier intersects privacy-posture; chronon-typed CBL + CloudKit shadow needs defender posture claim
Composes — shipping-app capability addition crosses storage boundary; lineage-preservation-plan applies

engineeringRecommendation.graduationStrategy: (c) hybrid-bridge — PollingDaemon writes-through to BOTH CoreData (current path, CloudKit-compat) AND chronon-typed CBL shadow store (substrate-typed queries enabled)
engineeringRecommendation.chrononVersion: v0.4.0 (latest; store-projection variant benchmarked in timeline-storage-matrix)
engineeringRecommendation.storageBackend: chronon-v000-004-000-store-projection via Fleece+CBL per timeline-storage-matrix benchmark
engineeringRecommendation.schemaBindingShape: TauQuoteMoment ← chronon-schemas.MomentModel v0.4.0 ← Fleece+CBL store-projection backend
engineeringRecommendation.retentionPosture: tiered — last 30 days hot (live-query CBL); 30-365 days warm (CBL reduced indexes); >1 year cold (Fleece-archived projections)
engineeringRecommendation.privacyPosture: local-only files for prototype-bound recording (substrate-honest per [[adversarial-audience-the-entity]]); CoreData CloudKit sync remains as user-facing path; chronon-typed shadow store is local-only
engineeringRecommendation.uiComponentLift: PriceScatterView (constraint-selection-lab/MatrixViews/) + stock-template-guide candlestick chart module — both read chronon-typed records via TauHistoryKit bridge

ldtProofsShape.preCodeLdtCommitment: Per [[Login Driven Thinking (LDT)]] — every structural claim above lands as a Swift Testing test in TauHistoryKit/tests/. Round-trip tests (CoreData QuoteHistoryTick ↔ chronon-typed QuoteMoment ↔ Fleece-CBL store) are required.
ldtProofsShape.estimatedSuiteCount: 5-6 suites: TauQuoteMomentTests + ChrononBridgeTests + PollingDaemonWriteThroughTests + RetentionTierTests + LiftedViewIntegrationTests

productManagerSpinUp.perDoctrine: [[non-concrete-definitions-trigger-product-manager-spin-up]] — same PM as tau-prototype (creative-selection-pattern-spawner extended with time-series-recording role-class)
openOperatorCreativeSelectionQuestions: 1. Confirm engineering recommendation (c) hybrid-bridge OR direct full-adopt-chronon-typed OR extend-existing-CoreData-only?
2. Confirm chronon-schemas v0.4.0 store-projection variant — OR walk the 9-variant timeline-storage-matrix benchmark first?
3. Recording scope — all symbols polled OR subset (per watchlist) OR all + retention-tier filtering?
4. Sampling rate — every PollingDaemon tick OR throttled (e.g., 1/sec, 1/min)?
5. Lift the recorded data into LIT prototype (matrix-solver verdict view consumes price history) NOW or DEFER to LIT prototype build?

launchReviewGate.perDoctrine: [[release-gate-audience-review]] — UI surfacing recording (e.g., 'History' tab; chart in matrix-solver verdict view) gates the launch review; recording-daemon-only work has lighter audience-review burden

maintenanceHandoff.primaryConsumer: Tau's existing AI helpers (StrategyAuditor + WhisperExit + TradeJournaler) gain typed price-history access; LIT prototype's matrix-solver verdict view consumes the same chronon-typed store
maintenanceHandoff.supersessionPolicy: Hybrid-bridge graduation path: when LIT-prototype validates + Tau's UI graduates to chronon-typed reads, retire CoreData QuoteHistoryTick entity. CoreData schema preserved as historical-data per [[preserve-historical-data]].

monitoringPosture.compileRegressions: TauHistoryKit + tau-app builds in CI; PollingDaemon write-through must not break existing CoreData CloudKit sync
monitoringPosture.dataRegressions: Per-tick round-trip tests verify chronon-typed write matches CoreData write within tolerance; retention-tier migration tests verify no data loss across hot→warm→cold transitions
monitoringPosture.performanceRegressions: PollingDaemon write-through must not exceed N ms per tick (target: <1ms write-through overhead; benchmark via timeline-storage-matrix's existing performance fixtures)
